rulings.Case Title: LUMBERMAN CONSTRUCTION SUPPLY, INC. VS. 1990 FOLSOM HOUSING ASSOCIATES, LP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 29, 2019 line 2. Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction Hearing Required. By no later than 9:00 a.m. parties to file a joint statement describing the following: (1) Plaintiff's position regarding the scope, the timeline and the cost of the outstanding work required to be done to address the damage; (2) Defendant's position regarding the scope, the timeline and the costs of the of each item of repairs/work required to be done to address the damage. Parties to specifically identify which items of damage are agreed upon, which items of repairs are agreed upon, and which items are in dispute whether because Defendant's claim that they did not cause particular damage or because no such damage exists. The brief preferably should be in a table format, e.g. I. Work Required Per Plaintiff: Foundation; II. Work Required per Defendant: No foundation damage caused by Defendant; III. Plaintiff's estimated cost of repair now: $X; IV. Defendant's estimated cost of repair now: $Y; V. Plaintiff's estimated costs of repair if Defendant's project proceeds: $A; VI. Defendant's estimated costs of repair if Defendant's project proceeds; $B; VII. Plaintiff's Timeline for repair now: X number of days; VIII. Defendant's Timeline for repairs now: Y number of days; IX. Plaintiff's Timeline for repair if Defendant's project proceeds: Z number of days; X. Defendant's Timeline for repairs if Defendant's project proceeds: W number of days. If brief is not submitted in the table format, the information outlined above should be contained in the brief in any other format chosen by the parties. The Court is inclined to order the repairs (some or all) to proceed before Defendant's project moves forward. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: CHP FIFTH STREET, LLC VS. SARAH CRONK ET AL
